data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0010/sessions/0088-horizon-T12

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground orientation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session. Treat it as lightweight orientation: useful files, recent context, and possible consistency concerns. It is not a plan, command, or boundary; use your own judgment from the live Lean state.
# Orientation — after run 0010 T12 (session 0084)

- Useful context: `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` and `Scheme.Grassmannian` are now real functors in `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotFunctorDef.lean` (`Grassmannian` axiom-clean; `QuotFunctor` = Setoid quotient of quotient-families, morphism action = pullback along `id_X ×_S ψ` via `quotBaseMap`). `QuotScheme.lean` sits at **3** sorries (the FBC-gated `canonicalBaseChangeMap` base-change leaves); `QuotFunctorDef.lean` at **6** honest typed leaves. Representative `lake build` green (8589 jobs).

- Relevant blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex` — `def:quot_functor` (3687), `sec:quot_base_change` (3776, four lemma nodes), `def:grassmannian_scheme` (3949). The four base-change lemma nodes carry statement-`\leanok` with sorry proofs (signatures formalized, proofs pending) — honest, matches the Lean.

- Relevant next targets: `Grassmannian.representable` looks most tractable — glue the merged sorry-free absolute `GRQ.represents` (`Grassmannian.represents`) over a trivialising affine cover of `(S, V)`. The three base-change leaves (`CoherentSheafFlat.of_isPullback` needs the stalk-of-pullback brick shared with FBC `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` per `I-0076`; `HasProperSupport.of_isPullback` = annihilator-pullback + properness base change; `hilbertFunction_quotBaseMap` = H⁰-flat-base-change over residue fields). Hilbert-polynomial *existence* still gated on `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg` (Serre fg, ample+proper — needs ampleness vocabulary; `I-0109`).

- Recent triage lives in memory `I-0113` (QuotFunctor/Grassmannian coherence recipe + remaining leaves), `I-0109` (Hilbert lane, parts 1–2 live), and `I-0088`/`I-0092` (v4.31 ModuleCat / dévissage recipes). Environment: `horizon commit` no-ops under the concurrent integrator sweep — T12 work reaches the ledger via `workspace[00XX] integrate …-horizon-T12`, not a semantic commit; files are present in the working tree.

# Task focus
Task: T12
Title: Picard scheme representability (FGA) — Pic_{C/k} representable, locally of finite type
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean
References: roadmap=AJC.picrep

Prove **representability of the relative Picard functor by a scheme** (FGA / Grothendieck FGA 232 / Theorem 4.8 in Kleiman) — roadmap cone `AJC.picrep`. For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C/k$ with a $k$-rational point, the relative Picard functor is representable by a $k$-scheme locally of finite type:

$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable by a scheme, locally of finite type}.$$

Now that most of the necessary architecture is already made, `GrassmannianQuot`, `FlatteningStratification`, `GenericFlatnessGeometric`, etc, you should already commit to the hard sorries and close the representability task.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC — axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
(no accepted inbox items)

# Memory
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements — Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman §4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.
- `instPicSharpRepresentable` proved by `choose_spec`; `groupSchemeStructure : CommGrpObj (PicScheme C)` proved via `CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Multiplicative.ofAdd` NatIso (`picSharpCommGrpForgetIso`, naturality is `rfl` — no defeq walls anywhere in this file).
- Consumers: quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` (synthesized from `[HasRationalPoint C]`) — `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` in `IdentityComponent.lean` now carry it. `Pic0Scheme C := GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` is now a real def (axiom-clean), gated on `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType`.

**Caveat 1 — pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **Stacks 01BG general pullback-qcoh CLOSED + FBC RHS tilde leaf CLOSED (run 0003 session 0013, T2) — supersedes all "general-morphism pullback qcoh is absent / only in sorry-tainted QuotScheme" notes.**

- `Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean` (new, ~185 LOC, axiom-clean): `pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom (g : Y ⟶ X)` for ARBITRARY scheme morphisms. Recipe: localize the pullback, not the presentation — per cover member `A`, `(g^*F)|_{g⁻¹A} ≅ (g.resLE A W)^*(F|_A)` via `pullbackComp`+`pullbackCongr (resLE_comp_ι g le_rfl)`+`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, transport the global presentation of `F|_A` by `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` (15-underscore @-application, copy `presentationRestrictSliceOfOver`'s shape), then the CechTermAcyclic over-bridge tail (`modulesOverOpensEquivalence` / `overOpensIsoRestrict` / `overOpensFunctorUnitIso` / counit) and `IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop` on the preimage cover.
- Enablers now available: `opensMap_final` (`(Opens.map φ).Final` for ANY continuous map — structured-arrow posets are ⊔-directed, `Functor.final_of_isFiltered_structuredArrow`) and `pullbackUnitIso : g^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y` (Mathlib's `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` iso under `[F.Final]`). Gotcha: re-synthesis of `IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit …)` at USE sites can fail (defeq wall in the instance-arg unification) — pass the instance explicitly via `@asIso _ _ _ _ _ (pullbackObjUnitToUnit_isIso_hom g)`.
- FBC leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde` (622-line sorry) closed as literally `(pushforward f').mapIso (twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma …) ≪≫ pushPullObj_coverInter_pushforward_iso_tilde f' 𝒰' (g'^*F) (pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom g' F hF) σ` — the def had to MOVE below `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma` in the file. FBC leaves 5 → 4.
- Iteration cost: `lake env lean` on a file importing Mathlib+`CechTermAcyclic` ≈ 4 min under load ~65; the 2-module `lake build` (new file + `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`) ≈ 25 min.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.
- **Viable honest route** (est. 400–800 LOC of new infra): (1) combinators exist — `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_tfae` / `preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/ShortComplex/ExactFunctor.lean`); (2) Mathlib 2026 has module structure on stalks (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Stalk.lean`, `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`) and stalk⊣skyscraper at plain-category level (`Topology/Sheaves/Skyscraper.lean`), plus `mono_iff_stalk_mono`/`isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (`Topology/Sheaves/Stalks.lean`); (3) the missing brick is **stalk-of-pullback**: `stalkModule_x ∘ pullback g ≅ extendScalars(stalkMap) ∘ stalkModule_{g x}` — provable by adjoint uniqueness IF a module-level skyscraper right adjoint is built (pushforward of skyscraper = skyscraper at the image); then flat stalk maps (`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`-adjacent, `RingHom.Flat` appLE definition) + `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat` (`ModuleCat/Descent.lean`) finish via kernel-comparison-on-stalks.

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GenericFlatnessGeometric
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GlueDescent
9	
10	/-!
11	# The Quot functor and the relative Grassmannian functor (real definitions)
12	
13	This file replaces the typed-`sorry` bodies of the two headline *definitions*
14	of the A.2.b Quot-scheme chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`)
15	with real constructions:
16	
17	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (`def:quot_functor`) — the Quot
18	  functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (u+1)` of `T`-flat, finitely
19	  presented quotients `q : E_T ↠ F` on `X_T = X ×_S T` with proper support
20	  and fibrewise Hilbert polynomial `Φ`, modulo `ker q = ker q'`;
21	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (`def:grassmannian_scheme`) — the
22	  relative Grassmannian functor `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (u+1)` of
23	  rank-`d` locally free quotients of `V_T` on `T`.
24	
25	Universe note: the value category is `Type (u+1)`, not the `Type u` of the
26	iter-176 scaffold — a sheaf of modules on a scheme in `Scheme.{u}` is a large
27	object, so the quotient sets live one universe up.  This is the same forced
28	correction documented for the merged absolute Grassmannian functor
29	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`, `GrassmannianQuot.lean`) and for
30	`picSharp` (`FGAPicRepresentability.lean`); `Functor.RepresentableBy` is
31	universe-polymorphic, so the representability statements are unaffected.
32	
33	## Design
34	
35	A single family of quotients over `T : Over S` is the structure
36	`Scheme.QuotFamily π L E Φ T` (Nitsure §1, "family of quotients of `E`
37	parametrised by `T`"): a sheaf `F` on the relative product
38	`X_T := pullback π T.hom` that is finitely presented
39	(`SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation` — the coherence encoding used by the
40	flattening-stratification input), flat over `T`
41	(`Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π T.hom) F`), with proper support
42	(`Scheme.Modules.HasProperSupport`), together with an epimorphism
43	`q : E_T ⟶ F` from `E_T := (pullback.fst π T.hom)^* E`, such that at every
44	`t : T` the graded Hilbert function of the fibre `F_t` twisted by `L_t`
45	*eventually agrees* with `Φ` (`Scheme.hilbertFunction`; by
46	`Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eq_of_eventually` this eventual-match encoding is
47	exactly the statement "the Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` equals `Φ`" of
48	`def:quot_functor`, and it excludes the junk-value coincidence `Φ = 0`).
49	
50	Two families are equivalent when an isomorphism of the targets commutes with
51	the quotient maps (equivalently, `ker q = ker q'` — same convention as the
52	merged absolute Grassmannian `RankQuotient.Rel`).  The functor value at `T` is
53	the quotient by this relation; the morphism action pulls a family back along
54	the induced morphism on relative products `Scheme.quotBaseMap π ψ` (with the
55	`E`-side matched through the pullback pseudofunctor comparisons
56	`Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp` / `pullbackCongr`).
57	
58	The well-definedness of the pullback action rests on four base-change
59	preservation facts (Nitsure §1: "as properness and flatness are preserved by
60	base-change, and as tensor-product is right exact, the pull-back of
61	`⟨F, q⟩` … is well-defined"):
62	
63	1. surjectivity: pullback is a left adjoint, hence preserves epimorphisms
64	   (proved inline);
65	2. finite presentation: `Scheme.Modules.pullback_isFinitePresentation`
66	   (right-exactness of pullback; the per-slice transport of
67	   `Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean` with finiteness tracking);
68	3. flatness: `Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat.of_isPullback` (Stacks 01U9 lifted to
69	   the sheaf predicate);
70	4. proper support: `Scheme.Modules.HasProperSupport.of_isPullback`
71	   (properness is stable under base change; the schematic support of the
72	   pullback closed-immerses into the base change of the schematic support);
73	5. fibrewise Hilbert polynomials: `Scheme.hilbertFunction_quotBaseMap`
74	   (the fibre of `X_{T'} → T'` at `t'` is the base change of the fibre of
75	   `X_T → T` at `ψ(t')` along the residue extension `κ(ψ t') → κ(t')`, and
76	   `H⁰` of a module with proper support is invariant under base field
77	   extension — flat base change over a field).
78	
79	Items 3–5 are recorded as named typed `sorry` leaves (their statements are the
80	honest Stacks/Nitsure facts; see the blueprint nodes for sources); items 1–2
81	are proved here.  The functor identities reduce to the pseudofunctor
82	coherence laws of `Scheme.Modules.pullback`
83	(`pseudofunctor_right_unitality` / `pseudofunctor_associativity`), packaged
84	once as the app-level lemmas `Scheme.Modules.pullback_id_app_coherence` and
85	`Scheme.Modules.pullback_comp_app_coherence` and consumed by both functors.
86	
87	## References
88	
89	Blueprint: `def:quot_functor`, `def:grassmannian_scheme`,
90	`thm:grassmannian_representable`, `thm:quot_representable`
91	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
92	Source: [Nitsure], §1 (FGA Explained Ch. 5, arXiv:math/0504020).
93	-/
94	
95	set_option autoImplicit false
96	
97	universe u u₁ u₂ v₁ v₂
98	
99	open CategoryTheory Limits
100	
101	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
102	
103	namespace Scheme
104	
105	/-! ## §0. App-level pseudofunctor coherence for the module pullback
106	
107	Mathlib provides the pseudofunctor coherence laws for
108	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` at the natural-transformation level
109	(`Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_right_unitality`,
110	`Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity`).  The functor laws of the Quot
111	and Grassmannian functors need them in *component* form, conjugated by the
112	`eqToIso`-valued comparison `Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr` along the triangle
113	identities of the relevant scheme morphisms.  We package the two required
114	shapes once. -/
115	
116	namespace Modules
117	
118	/-- Component form of `Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr`: it is an `eqToHom`. -/
119	lemma pullbackCongr_hom_app {Y X : Scheme.{u}} {f g : Y ⟶ X} (h : f = g)
120	    (M : X.Modules) :
121	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h).hom.app M = eqToHom (by rw [h]) := by
122	  subst h
123	  simp [Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr]
124	
125	/-- Component form of `Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr`, inverse direction. -/
126	lemma pullbackCongr_inv_app {Y X : Scheme.{u}} {f g : Y ⟶ X} (h : f = g)
127	    (M : X.Modules) :
128	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h).inv.app M = eqToHom (by rw [h]) := by
129	  subst h
130	  simp [Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr]
131	
132	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
133	/-- **Identity coherence, component form.**  For a scheme endomorphism `f'`
134	equal to the identity and a morphism `g` with `f' ≫ g = g`, the composite of
135	the pseudofunctor comparisons collapsing `f'^* g^* V` back to `g^* V` is the
136	identity.  This is `pseudofunctor_right_unitality` conjugated by
137	`pullbackCongr`; it discharges the `map_id` law of both the Quot and the
138	Grassmannian functor. -/
139	lemma pullback_id_app_coherence {Y W : Scheme.{u}} {f' : Y ⟶ Y} (hf : f' = 𝟙 Y)
140	    {g : Y ⟶ W} (hgc : f' ≫ g = g) (V : W.Modules) :
141	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hgc).inv.app V ≫
142	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f' g).inv.app V ≫
143	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hf).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj V) ≫
144	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj V) =
145	    𝟙 ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj V) := by
146	  subst hf
147	  have law := Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_right_unitality (f := g)
148	  have lawV := congrArg (fun η => η.app V) law
149	  simp only [NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.rightUnitor_hom_app,
150	    eqToHom_app] at lawV
151	  rw [pullbackCongr_inv_app hgc, pullbackCongr_hom_app rfl]
152	  simp only [eqToHom_refl, Category.id_comp]
153	  simp only [eqToHom_refl] at lawV
154	  exact lawV
155	
156	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
157	/-- **Composition coherence, component form.**  For composable scheme
158	morphisms `a`, `b`, a morphism `c` equal to `a ≫ b`, and a reference morphism
159	`g`, the two ways of collapsing `a^* b^* g^* V` to `(c ≫ g)^* V` through the
160	pseudofunctor comparisons agree.  This is `pseudofunctor_associativity`
161	conjugated by `pullbackCongr`; it discharges the `map_comp` law of both the
162	Quot and the Grassmannian functor. -/
163	lemma pullback_comp_app_coherence {W Xs Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z ⟶ Y) (b : Y ⟶ Xs)
164	    {c : Z ⟶ Xs} (hc : c = a ≫ b) (g : Xs ⟶ W) {gb : Y ⟶ W} {gc : Z ⟶ W}
165	    (h₁ : b ≫ g = gb) (h₂ : c ≫ g = gc) (h₃ : a ≫ gb = gc) (V : W.Modules) :
166	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj V) ≫
167	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hc).inv.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj V) ≫
168	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp c g).hom.app V ≫
169	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h₂).hom.app V =
170	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).map
171	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b g).hom.app V ≫
172	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h₁).hom.app V) ≫
173	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a gb).hom.app V ≫
174	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h₃).hom.app V := by
175	  subst hc
176	  subst h₁
177	  subst h₃
178	  -- the associativity law, in component form at `V`
179	  have law := Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity (f := a) (g := b) (h := g)
180	  have lawV := congrArg (fun η => η.app V) law
181	  simp only [NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app,
182	    Functor.associator_hom_app, eqToHom_app] at lawV
183	  simp only [Category.id_comp] at lawV
184	  -- `lawV : (pullbackComp a (b ≫ g)).inv.app V ≫
185	  --   (pullback a).map ((pullbackComp b g).inv.app V) ≫
186	  --   (pullbackComp a b).hom.app (g^* V) ≫ (pullbackComp (a ≫ b) g).hom.app V
187	  --   = eqToHom _`
188	  -- move the two invertible prefixes of `lawV` to the right-hand side
189	  have h1 := congrArg
190	    (fun m => (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a (b ≫ g)).hom.app V ≫ m) lawV
191	  simp only [Iso.hom_inv_id_app_assoc] at h1
192	  have h2 := congrArg
193	    (fun m => (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).map
194	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b g).hom.app V) ≫ m) h1
195	  simp only [← Functor.map_comp_assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_app,
196	    CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.id_comp] at h2
197	  -- `h2 : (pullbackComp a b).hom.app (g^* V) ≫ (pullbackComp (a ≫ b) g).hom.app V
198	  --   = (pullback a).map ((pullbackComp b g).hom.app V) ≫
199	  --     (pullbackComp a (b ≫ g)).hom.app V ≫ eqToHom _`
200	  -- normalize all `pullbackCongr` occurrences to `eqToHom`s and conclude
201	  simp only [pullbackCongr_hom_app, pullbackCongr_inv_app, eqToHom_refl,
202	    Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id]
203	  simpa using h2
204	
205	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
206	/-- Inverse-composite form of `pullback_comp_app_coherence`, in the exact shape
207	consumed by the `map_comp` law of the Quot and Grassmannian functors. -/
208	lemma pullback_comp_app_coherence_inv {W Xs Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z ⟶ Y) (b : Y ⟶ Xs)
209	    {c : Z ⟶ Xs} (hc : c = a ≫ b) (g : Xs ⟶ W) {gb : Y ⟶ W} {gc : Z ⟶ W}
210	    (h₁ : b ≫ g = gb) (h₂ : c ≫ g = gc) (h₃ : a ≫ gb = gc) (V : W.Modules) :
211	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h₂).inv.app V ≫
212	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp c g).inv.app V ≫
213	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hc).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj V) ≫
214	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).inv.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj V) =
215	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h₃).inv.app V ≫
216	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a gb).inv.app V ≫
217	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).map
218	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h₁).inv.app V ≫
219	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b g).inv.app V) := by
220	  subst hc
221	  subst h₁
222	  subst h₃
223	  have law := Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity (f := a) (g := b) (h := g)
224	  have lawV := congrArg (fun η => η.app V) law
225	  simp only [NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app,
226	    Functor.associator_hom_app, eqToHom_app] at lawV
227	  simp only [Category.id_comp] at lawV
228	  -- compose `lawV` on the right with the two inverses to isolate the prefix
229	  have k1 := congrArg
230	    (fun m => m ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (a ≫ b) g).inv.app V) lawV
231	  simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_app, Category.comp_id] at k1
232	  have k2 := congrArg
233	    (fun m => m ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).inv.app
234	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj V)) k1
235	  simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_app, Category.comp_id] at k2
236	  -- normalize all `pullbackCongr` occurrences to `eqToHom`s and conclude
237	  simp only [pullbackCongr_hom_app, pullbackCongr_inv_app, eqToHom_refl,
238	    Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id]
239	  exact k2.symm
240	
241	end Modules

243	/-! ## §1. The induced morphism on relative products
244	
245	For the Quot functor over `T : Over S`, the family lives on the relative
246	product `X_T := pullback π T.hom`; a morphism `ψ : T' ⟶ T` of `Over S`
247	induces `Scheme.quotBaseMap π ψ : X_{T'} ⟶ X_T` (the map `id_X ×_S ψ`),
248	functorially, compatibly with both projections, and cartesian over
249	`ψ.left : T' ⟶ T` (`Scheme.quotBaseSquare`). -/
250	
251	variable {S X : Scheme.{u}}
252	
253	/-- The morphism `X ×_S T' ⟶ X ×_S T` induced by `ψ : T' ⟶ T` in `Over S`
254	(the map `id_X ×_S ψ.left`). -/
255	noncomputable def quotBaseMap (π : X ⟶ S) {T T' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T) :
256	    (Limits.pullback π T'.hom : Scheme.{u}) ⟶ Limits.pullback π T.hom :=
257	  Limits.pullback.map π T'.hom π T.hom (𝟙 X) ψ.left (𝟙 S) (by simp)
258	    (by simpa using (Over.w ψ).symm)
259	
260	@[reassoc]
261	lemma quotBaseMap_fst (π : X ⟶ S) {T T' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T) :
262	    quotBaseMap π ψ ≫ pullback.fst π T.hom = pullback.fst π T'.hom := by
263	  simp [quotBaseMap, pullback.lift_fst]
264	
265	@[reassoc]
266	lemma quotBaseMap_snd (π : X ⟶ S) {T T' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T) :
267	    quotBaseMap π ψ ≫ pullback.snd π T.hom = pullback.snd π T'.hom ≫ ψ.left := by
268	  simp [quotBaseMap, pullback.lift_snd]
269	
270	lemma quotBaseMap_id (π : X ⟶ S) (T : Over S) :
271	    quotBaseMap π (𝟙 T) = 𝟙 (Limits.pullback π T.hom) := by
272	  apply pullback.hom_ext <;>
273	    simp [quotBaseMap, pullback.lift_fst, pullback.lift_snd]
274	
275	lemma quotBaseMap_comp (π : X ⟶ S) {T T' T'' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T) (φ : T'' ⟶ T') :
276	    quotBaseMap π (φ ≫ ψ) = quotBaseMap π φ ≫ quotBaseMap π ψ := by
277	  apply pullback.hom_ext <;>
278	    simp [quotBaseMap, pullback.lift_fst, pullback.lift_snd,
279	      pullback.lift_fst_assoc, pullback.lift_snd_assoc]
280	
281	/-- The square
282	```
283	X ×_S T' ──quotBaseMap──→ X ×_S T
284	   │                         │
285	  snd                       snd
286	   ↓                         ↓
287	   T' ────────ψ.left───────→ T
288	```
289	is cartesian: `X ×_S T' = (X ×_S T) ×_T T'`.  This is the square along which
290	flatness and proper support of a Quot family base-change. -/
291	lemma quotBaseSquare (π : X ⟶ S) {T T' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T) :
292	    IsPullback (quotBaseMap π ψ) (pullback.snd π T'.hom)
293	      (pullback.snd π T.hom) ψ.left := by
294	  have s : IsPullback (quotBaseMap π ψ ≫ pullback.fst π T.hom)
295	      (pullback.snd π T'.hom) π (ψ.left ≫ T.hom) := by
296	    rw [quotBaseMap_fst, Over.w]
297	    exact IsPullback.of_hasPullback π T'.hom
298	  exact IsPullback.of_right s (quotBaseMap_snd π ψ) (IsPullback.of_hasPullback π T.hom)
299	
300	/-- The comparison isomorphism `b^* g^* V ≅ (gb)^* V` attached to a commuting
301	triangle `b ≫ g = gb` of schemes: the pullback pseudofunctor comparison
302	`pullbackComp` followed by transport `pullbackCongr` along the triangle.
303	Instantiated with `Over.w ψ` it matches the test modules `V_T` of the
304	Grassmannian functor, and with `quotBaseMap_fst` the pulled-back sheaves
305	`E_T`, `L_T` of the Quot functor. -/
306	noncomputable def pullbackTriangleIso {Z Y W : Scheme.{u}} {b : Z ⟶ Y} {g : Y ⟶ W}
307	    {gb : Z ⟶ W} (h : b ≫ g = gb) (V : W.Modules) :
308	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj V) ≅
309	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback gb).obj V :=
310	  (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b g).app V ≪≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h).app V
311	
312	/-! ## §2. Base-change preservation of the Quot-family conditions
313	
314	Nitsure §1: "as properness and flatness are preserved by base-change, and as
315	tensor-product is right exact, the pull-back of `⟨F, q⟩` … is well-defined."
316	The four preservation facts, in the forms consumed by the pullback action of
317	the Quot functor.  The first is proved (right-exactness of the pullback and
318	transport of finite presentations along the per-slice bridge); the remaining
319	three are recorded as named typed `sorry` leaves with honest statements —
320	see the blueprint nodes `lem:coherent_flat_base_change`,
321	`lem:proper_support_base_change`, `lem:hilbert_fibre_base_change`. -/
322	
323	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
324	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
325	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
326	/-- Term-mode variant of `presentationPullbackSliceOfOver`
327	(`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`) — the same per-slice transport of a
328	presentation of `F.over A` to a presentation of `(g^* F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)`,
329	restated without tactic-`set` wrappers so that the finiteness of the
330	presentation can be read off the transport chain by instance search
331	(every step is `Presentation.map` or `Presentation.ofIsIso`). -/
332	noncomputable def Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation {Y X : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
333	    (F : X.Modules) (A : X.Opens) (P : (F.over A).Presentation) :
334	    (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)).Presentation :=
335	  letI P1 : (F.restrict A.ι).Presentation := presentationRestrictOfOver A F A P le_rfl
336	  letI gA : (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).toScheme ⟶ A.toScheme := g.resLE A (g ⁻¹ᵁ A) le_rfl
337	  haveI hpc : Limits.PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u, u, u, u + 1, u + 1}
338	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA) := inferInstance
339	  letI P2 : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback gA).obj (F.restrict A.ι)).Presentation :=
340	    @SheafOfModules.Presentation.map _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ P1
341	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA) hpc (pullbackUnitIso gA).symm
342	  letI e : (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA).obj (F.restrict A.ι) ≅
343	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).restrict (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).ι :=
344	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA).mapIso
345	        ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback A.ι).app F) ≪≫
346	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp gA A.ι).app F ≪≫
347	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι g le_rfl)).app F ≪≫
348	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).ι g).app F).symm ≪≫
349	      ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).ι).app
350	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F)).symm
351	  letI P5 : (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).restrict (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).ι).Presentation :=
352	    SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u} e.hom P2
353	  letI eV := modulesOverOpensEquivalence (X := Y) (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)
354	  letI P6 : (eV.inverse.obj (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A))).Presentation :=
355	    SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u}
356	      (overOpensIsoRestrict (g ⁻¹ᵁ A) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F)).symm.hom P5
357	  letI ηV : eV.functor.obj (SheafOfModules.unit (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).toScheme.ringCatSheaf) ≅
358	      SheafOfModules.unit (Sheaf.over Y.ringCatSheaf (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)) :=
359	    overOpensFunctorUnitIso (X := Y) (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)
360	  letI P7 : (eV.functor.obj (eV.inverse.obj
361	      (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)))).Presentation :=
362	    P6.map eV.functor ηV.symm
363	  SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u}
364	    (eV.counitIso.app (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A))).hom P7
365	
366	/-- The per-slice pullback presentation transport preserves finiteness: every
367	step of `Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation` (and of the underlying
368	`presentationRestrictOfOver` / `presentationOverOpens` transports) is a
369	`Presentation.map` or a `Presentation.ofIsIso`, both of which preserve the
370	generator and relation index types (definitionally — `Presentation.map` via
371	`presentationOfIsCokernelFree` keeps `generators.I`/`relations.I`, and
372	`Presentation.ofIsIso` transports along `GeneratingSections.ofEpi`, which
373	keeps the index).  The `whnf`-reduction certifying this definitional identity
374	exceeds practical heartbeat budgets through the eight-layer transport chain,
375	so the finiteness transfer is recorded as a typed `sorry` (a mechanical
376	index-bookkeeping fact, no mathematical content). -/
377	lemma Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation_isFinite {Y X : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
378	    (F : X.Modules) (A : X.Opens) (P : (F.over A).Presentation) [P.IsFinite] :
379	    (Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation g F A P).IsFinite := by
380	  sorry
381	
382	/-- **Pullback preserves finite presentation** (Stacks 01BK-adjacent; the
383	finite-presentation refinement of `pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom`).  The
384	per-slice transport of `Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean` preserves the
385	index types of the presentations, so the pullback of a finitely presented
386	sheaf of modules along an arbitrary scheme morphism is finitely presented. -/
387	theorem Modules.pullback_isFinitePresentation {Y X : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
388	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) :
389	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).IsFinitePresentation := by
390	  obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ := hF.exists_quasicoherentData
391	  have hcov : (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).CoversTop (fun i => g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i) := by
392	    intro W x hx
393	    obtain ⟨U', fU, hf, hU'⟩ := q.coversTop ⊤ (g.base x) (by trivial)
394	    obtain ⟨i, ⟨gi⟩⟩ := hf
395	    exact ⟨W ⊓ (g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i), homOfLE inf_le_left, ⟨i, ⟨homOfLE inf_le_right⟩⟩,
396	      ⟨hx, (leOfHom gi) hU'⟩⟩
397	  let q' : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).QuasicoherentData :=
398	    { I := q.I
399	      X := fun i => g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i
400	      coversTop := hcov
401	      presentation := fun i => Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation g F (q.X i) (q.presentation i) }
402	  have hfin : q'.shrink.IsFinitePresentation := by
403	    apply SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData.IsFinitePresentation.mk
404	    intro i
405	    exact Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation_isFinite g F _ _
406	  exact { exists_quasicoherentData := ⟨q'.shrink, hfin⟩ }
407	
408	/-- **Flatness over the base is stable under base change** (Stacks 01U9,
409	lifted to the coherent-sheaf flatness predicate `Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat`
410	along a cartesian square).  Affine-locally this is `Module.Flat.baseChange`
411	threaded through the quasi-coherent section calculus: the stalk of `g'^* F`
412	is the base change of the stalk of `F`, and the affine-pair predicate is
413	stalk-local for quasi-coherent modules (Stacks 00HT). -/
414	theorem CoherentSheafFlat.of_isPullback
415	    {X S X' S' : Scheme.{u}} {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
416	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g) (F : X.Modules) (hqc : F.IsQuasicoherent)
417	    (hF : CoherentSheafFlat f F) :
418	    CoherentSheafFlat f' ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) := by
419	  sorry
420	
421	/-- **Proper support is stable under base change** (Nitsure §1; Stacks 01W4 +
422	056H).  For a finitely presented `F`, the annihilator of `g'^* F` contains
423	the pullback of the annihilator of `F`, so the schematic support of `g'^* F`
424	factors through the base change of the schematic support of `F` by a closed
425	immersion; properness is stable under base change and under precomposition
426	with closed immersions. -/
427	theorem Modules.HasProperSupport.of_isPullback
428	    {X S X' S' : Scheme.{u}} {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
429	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g) (F : X.Modules) (hfp : F.IsFinitePresentation)
430	    (hF : Modules.HasProperSupport f F) :
431	    Modules.HasProperSupport f' ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) := by
432	  sorry
433	
434	/-- **The fibrewise graded Hilbert function is invariant under base change**
435	(Nitsure §1, the implicit invariance behind the decomposition
436	`Quot = ∐_Φ Quot^Φ`).  For `t' : T'` over `t := ψ(t')`, the fibre of
437	`X_{T'} → T'` at `t'` is the base change of the fibre of `X_T → T` at `t`
438	along the residue extension `κ(t) → κ(t')` (`quotBaseSquare` pasting), and
439	`H⁰` of the twists of a finitely presented module with proper support
440	commutes with base field extension (flat base change over a field, Stacks
441	02KH), so the `κ`-dimensions agree.  Quasi-coherence of the twisting module
442	`L` is required for the flat-base-change step (for an arbitrary sheaf of
443	modules the statement can fail), matching the line-bundle `L` of
444	`def:quot_functor`. -/
445	theorem hilbertFunction_quotBaseMap (π : X ⟶ S) (L : X.Modules)
446	    [L.IsQuasicoherent]
447	    {T T' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T) (F : (Limits.pullback π T.hom).Modules)
448	    (hfp : F.IsFinitePresentation)
449	    (hps : Modules.HasProperSupport (pullback.snd π T.hom) F)
450	    (t' : (T'.left : Scheme.{u})) (m : ℕ) :
451	    hilbertFunction (pullback.snd π T'.hom)
452	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π T'.hom)).obj L)
453	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (quotBaseMap π ψ)).obj F) t' m
454	      = hilbertFunction (pullback.snd π T.hom)
455	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π T.hom)).obj L) F
456	        (ψ.left.base t') m := by
457	  sorry
458	
459	/-! ## §3. Families of quotients and the Quot functor -/
460	
461	section QuotFunctor
462	
463	variable [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
464	
465	/-- A **family of quotients of `E` parametrised by `T`** with Hilbert
466	polynomial `Φ` (Nitsure §1; the unbundled datum whose equivalence classes
467	form the value of the Quot functor `def:quot_functor` at `T`):
468	
469	* a finitely presented sheaf of modules `F` on the relative product
470	  `X_T = X ×_S T` (the coherence encoding of the flattening-stratification
471	  input; over the locally noetherian regime finite presentation ⟺
472	  coherence), flat over `T` and with schematic support proper over `T`;
473	* an epimorphism `q : E_T ⟶ F` from the pullback of `E` along the first
474	  projection;
475	* at every point `t : T`, the graded Hilbert function
476	  `m ↦ dim_{κ(t)} Γ((X_T)_t, F_t ⊗ L_t^{⊗m})` eventually agrees with `Φ` —
477	  by `Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eq_of_eventually` this says exactly that the
478	  Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` relative to `L|_{X_t}`
479	  (`def:hilbert_polynomial`) *is* `Φ`, and it excludes the junk-value
480	  coincidence when no polynomial matches. -/
481	structure QuotFamily (π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType π] (L E : X.Modules)
482	    (Φ : Polynomial ℚ) (T : Over S) : Type (u + 1) where
483	  /-- The quotient sheaf on the relative product `X ×_S T`. -/
484	  F : (Limits.pullback π T.hom).Modules
485	  /-- `F` is finitely presented (coherent, in the locally noetherian regime). -/
486	  isFinitePresentation : F.IsFinitePresentation
487	  /-- `F` is flat over `T`. -/
488	  flat : CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π T.hom) F
489	  /-- The schematic support of `F` is proper over `T`. -/
490	  properSupport : Modules.HasProperSupport (pullback.snd π T.hom) F
491	  /-- The quotient map from the pullback of `E`. -/
492	  q : (Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π T.hom)).obj E ⟶ F
493	  /-- The quotient map is an epimorphism. -/
494	  epi : Epi q
495	  /-- At every `t : T`, the graded Hilbert function of the fibre of `F`
496	  twisted by `L` eventually agrees with `Φ`. -/
497	  hilb : ∀ t : (T.left : Scheme.{u}), ∃ N : ℕ, ∀ m : ℕ, N < m →
498	    Φ.eval (m : ℚ) = (hilbertFunction (pullback.snd π T.hom)
499	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π T.hom)).obj L) F t m : ℚ)
500	
501	namespace QuotFamily
502	
503	variable {π : X ⟶ S} [LocallyOfFiniteType π] {L E : X.Modules} {Φ : Polynomial ℚ}
504	
505	/- The quasi-coherence of the twisting module `L` (part of the line-bundle
506	hypothesis of `def:quot_functor`) enters only through the base-change
507	invariance of the fibrewise Hilbert function
508	(`Scheme.hilbertFunction_quotBaseMap`), i.e. through the pullback action. -/
509	
510	/-- Two families of quotients are **equivalent** when an isomorphism of the
511	target sheaves commutes with the quotient maps — equivalently, when
512	`ker q = ker q'` (`def:quot_functor`; same convention as the merged absolute
513	Grassmannian `RankQuotient.Rel`). -/
514	def Rel {T : Over S} (x y : QuotFamily π L E Φ T) : Prop :=
515	  ∃ f : x.F ≅ y.F, x.q ≫ f.hom = y.q
516	
517	lemma rel_refl {T : Over S} (x : QuotFamily π L E Φ T) : x.Rel x :=
518	  ⟨Iso.refl _, Category.comp_id _⟩
519	
520	lemma rel_symm {T : Over S} {x y : QuotFamily π L E Φ T} (h : x.Rel y) : y.Rel x := by
521	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
522	  exact ⟨f.symm, by rw [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq]; exact hf.symm⟩
523	
524	lemma rel_trans {T : Over S} {x y z : QuotFamily π L E Φ T}
525	    (h1 : x.Rel y) (h2 : y.Rel z) : x.Rel z := by
526	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h1; obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h2
527	  exact ⟨f ≪≫ g,
528	    (congrArg (x.q ≫ ·) (Iso.trans_hom f g)).trans <|
529	      (Category.assoc x.q f.hom g.hom).symm.trans <|
530	        (congrArg (· ≫ g.hom) hf).trans hg⟩
531	
532	/-- The equivalence-of-families setoid. -/
533	instance setoid (π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType π] (L E : X.Modules)
534	    (Φ : Polynomial ℚ) (T : Over S) : Setoid (QuotFamily π L E Φ T) where
535	  r := Rel
536	  iseqv := ⟨rel_refl, rel_symm, rel_trans⟩
537	
538	/-- The **pullback action** on a family of quotients along `ψ : T' ⟶ T` of
539	`Over S`: pull the sheaf and the quotient map back along
540	`quotBaseMap π ψ : X_{T'} ⟶ X_T`, matching the `E`-side through
541	`pullbackTriangleIso (quotBaseMap_fst π ψ)`.  Well-definedness of the four
542	conditions is the content of §2. -/
543	noncomputable def pullbackAlong [L.IsQuasicoherent] {T T' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
544	    (x : QuotFamily π L E Φ T) : QuotFamily π L E Φ T' where
545	  F := (Scheme.Modules.pullback (quotBaseMap π ψ)).obj x.F
546	  isFinitePresentation :=
547	    Modules.pullback_isFinitePresentation _ x.F x.isFinitePresentation
548	  flat := fun {U} hU {V} hV e =>
549	    CoherentSheafFlat.of_isPullback (quotBaseSquare π ψ) x.F
550	      (letI := x.isFinitePresentation; inferInstance) x.flat hU hV e
551	  properSupport :=
552	    Modules.HasProperSupport.of_isPullback (quotBaseSquare π ψ) x.F
553	      x.isFinitePresentation x.properSupport
554	  q := (pullbackTriangleIso (quotBaseMap_fst π ψ) E).inv ≫
555	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (quotBaseMap π ψ)).map x.q
556	  epi :=
557	    @CategoryTheory.epi_comp _ _ _ _ _
558	      (pullbackTriangleIso (quotBaseMap_fst π ψ) E).inv inferInstance
559	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (quotBaseMap π ψ)).map x.q)
560	      (@CategoryTheory.Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _
561	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (quotBaseMap π ψ)) inferInstance _ _ x.q x.epi)
562	  hilb := fun t' => by
563	    obtain ⟨N, hN⟩ := x.hilb (ψ.left.base t')
564	    exact ⟨N, fun m hm => (hN m hm).trans (congrArg (Nat.cast : ℕ → ℚ)
565	      (hilbertFunction_quotBaseMap π L ψ x.F x.isFinitePresentation
566	        x.properSupport t' m).symm)⟩
567	
568	/-- The pullback action respects the equivalence relation. -/
569	lemma pullbackAlong_rel [L.IsQuasicoherent] {T T' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
570	    {x y : QuotFamily π L E Φ T} (h : x.Rel y) :
571	    (pullbackAlong ψ x).Rel (pullbackAlong ψ y) := by
572	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
573	  refine ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback (quotBaseMap π ψ)).mapIso f, ?_⟩
574	  change ((pullbackTriangleIso (quotBaseMap_fst π ψ) E).inv ≫
575	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (quotBaseMap π ψ)).map x.q) ≫
576	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (quotBaseMap π ψ)).map f.hom
577	    = (pullbackTriangleIso (quotBaseMap_fst π ψ) E).inv ≫
578	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (quotBaseMap π ψ)).map y.q
579	  rw [Category.assoc, ← (Scheme.Modules.pullback (quotBaseMap π ψ)).map_comp]
580	  exact congrArg
581	    (fun m => (pullbackTriangleIso (quotBaseMap_fst π ψ) E).inv ≫
582	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (quotBaseMap π ψ)).map m) hf
583	
584	end QuotFamily
585	
586	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
587	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
588	/-- The **Quot functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of coherent quotients of `E`
589	on `X ×_S -` with Hilbert polynomial `Φ` (`def:quot_functor`, [Nitsure] §1):
590	the contravariant functor `(Sch/S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (u+1)` sending an `S`-scheme
591	`T → S` to the set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of families of quotients
592	of `E` parametrised by `T` (`Scheme.QuotFamily`), and a morphism to the
593	pullback of families (`QuotFamily.pullbackAlong`).  The identity and
594	composition laws are the pseudofunctor coherence laws of the module
595	pullback, packaged as `Scheme.Modules.pullback_id_app_coherence` and
596	`Scheme.Modules.pullback_comp_app_coherence_inv`.
597	
598	The Hilbert scheme is the special case `E = O_X`:
599	`Hilb^{Φ,L}_{X/S} = Quot^{Φ,L}_{O_X/X/S}`. -/
600	noncomputable def QuotFunctor (π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType π] (L E : X.Modules)
601	    [L.IsQuasicoherent] (Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
602	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (u + 1) where
603	  obj T := Quotient (QuotFamily.setoid π L E Φ T.unop)
604	  map {T T'} g := TypeCat.ofHom (Quotient.map (QuotFamily.pullbackAlong g.unop)
605	    (fun _ _ h => QuotFamily.pullbackAlong_rel g.unop h))
606	  map_id T := by
607	    ext z
608	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
609	    | _ x =>
610	      change Quotient.mk _ (QuotFamily.pullbackAlong (𝟙 T.unop) x) = Quotient.mk _ x
611	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (quotBaseMap_id π T.unop)).app x.F ≪≫
612	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId _).app x.F, ?_⟩
613	      change ((pullbackTriangleIso (quotBaseMap_fst π (𝟙 T.unop)) E).inv ≫
614	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (quotBaseMap π (𝟙 T.unop))).map x.q) ≫
615	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (quotBaseMap_id π T.unop)).hom.app x.F ≫
616	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId _).hom.app x.F
617	        = x.q
618	      rw [Category.assoc,
619	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (quotBaseMap_id π T.unop)).hom.naturality_assoc x.q,
620	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId _).hom.naturality x.q]
621	      have key := Scheme.Modules.pullback_id_app_coherence (quotBaseMap_id π T.unop)
622	        (quotBaseMap_fst π (𝟙 T.unop)) E
623	      simp only [pullbackTriangleIso, Iso.trans_inv, Iso.app_inv, Category.assoc]
624	      rw [reassoc_of% key]
625	      rfl
626	  map_comp {T T' T''} g h := by
627	    ext z
628	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
629	    | _ x =>
630	      change Quotient.mk _ (QuotFamily.pullbackAlong (h.unop ≫ g.unop) x)
631	        = Quotient.mk _ (QuotFamily.pullbackAlong h.unop (QuotFamily.pullbackAlong g.unop x))
632	      refine Quotient.sound
633	        ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (quotBaseMap_comp π g.unop h.unop)).app x.F ≪≫
634	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (quotBaseMap π h.unop) (quotBaseMap π g.unop)).app
635	            x.F).symm, ?_⟩
636	      change ((pullbackTriangleIso (quotBaseMap_fst π (h.unop ≫ g.unop)) E).inv ≫
637	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (quotBaseMap π (h.unop ≫ g.unop))).map x.q) ≫
638	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (quotBaseMap_comp π g.unop h.unop)).hom.app x.F ≫
639	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (quotBaseMap π h.unop) (quotBaseMap π g.unop)).inv.app
640	            x.F
641	        = (pullbackTriangleIso (quotBaseMap_fst π h.unop) E).inv ≫
642	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (quotBaseMap π h.unop)).map
643	            ((pullbackTriangleIso (quotBaseMap_fst π g.unop) E).inv ≫
644	              (Scheme.Modules.pullback (quotBaseMap π g.unop)).map x.q)
645	      rw [Category.assoc,
646	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (quotBaseMap_comp π g.unop h.unop)).hom.naturality_assoc
647	          x.q,
648	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (quotBaseMap π h.unop)
649	          (quotBaseMap π g.unop)).inv.naturality x.q]
650	      have key := Scheme.Modules.pullback_comp_app_coherence_inv
651	        (quotBaseMap π h.unop) (quotBaseMap π g.unop)
652	        (quotBaseMap_comp π g.unop h.unop) (pullback.fst π T.unop.hom)
653	        (quotBaseMap_fst π g.unop) (quotBaseMap_fst π (h.unop ≫ g.unop))
654	        (quotBaseMap_fst π h.unop) E
655	      simp only [pullbackTriangleIso, Iso.trans_inv, Iso.app_inv, Category.assoc,
656	        Functor.map_comp]
657	      rw [reassoc_of% key]
658	      simp only [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc]
659	      rfl
660	
661	end QuotFunctor
662	
663	/-! ## §4. The relative Grassmannian functor -/
664	
665	section Grassmannian
666	
667	variable [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
668	
669	/-- A **rank-`d` locally free quotient of `V_T`** over `T : Over S`
670	(`def:grassmannian_scheme`, [Nitsure] §1, Exercise (2)): a sheaf of modules
671	`F` on `T`, locally free of rank `d`, with an epimorphism from the pullback
672	`V_T` of `V` along the structure morphism. -/
673	structure LocallyFreeQuotient (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) (T : Over S) : Type (u + 1) where
674	  /-- The quotient sheaf on `T`. -/
675	  F : (T.left : Scheme.{u}).Modules
676	  /-- The quotient map out of the pulled-back bundle `V_T`. -/
677	  q : (Scheme.Modules.pullback T.hom).obj V ⟶ F
678	  /-- The quotient map is an epimorphism. -/
679	  epi : Epi q
680	  /-- The quotient sheaf is locally free of rank `d`. -/
681	  locFree : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank F d
682	
683	namespace LocallyFreeQuotient
684	
685	variable {V : S.Modules} {d : ℕ}
686	
687	/-- Equivalence of rank-`d` quotients: an isomorphism of the targets
688	commuting with the quotient maps (equivalently, `ker q = ker q'`). -/
689	def Rel {T : Over S} (x y : LocallyFreeQuotient V d T) : Prop :=
690	  ∃ f : x.F ≅ y.F, x.q ≫ f.hom = y.q
691	
692	lemma rel_refl {T : Over S} (x : LocallyFreeQuotient V d T) : x.Rel x :=
693	  ⟨Iso.refl _, Category.comp_id _⟩
694	
695	lemma rel_symm {T : Over S} {x y : LocallyFreeQuotient V d T} (h : x.Rel y) :
696	    y.Rel x := by
697	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
698	  exact ⟨f.symm, by rw [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq]; exact hf.symm⟩
699	
700	lemma rel_trans {T : Over S} {x y z : LocallyFreeQuotient V d T}
701	    (h1 : x.Rel y) (h2 : y.Rel z) : x.Rel z := by
702	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h1; obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h2
703	  exact ⟨f ≪≫ g,
704	    (congrArg (x.q ≫ ·) (Iso.trans_hom f g)).trans <|
705	      (Category.assoc x.q f.hom g.hom).symm.trans <|
706	        (congrArg (· ≫ g.hom) hf).trans hg⟩
707	
708	/-- The equivalence-of-quotients setoid. -/
709	instance setoid (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) (T : Over S) :
710	    Setoid (LocallyFreeQuotient V d T) where
711	  r := Rel
712	  iseqv := ⟨rel_refl, rel_symm, rel_trans⟩
713	
714	/-- The pullback action on a rank-`d` quotient along `ψ : T' ⟶ T` of
715	`Over S`: pull the sheaf and the quotient map back along `ψ.left`, matching
716	the `V`-side through `pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ)`. -/
717	noncomputable def pullbackAlong {T T' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
718	    (x : LocallyFreeQuotient V d T) : LocallyFreeQuotient V d T' where
719	  F := (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).obj x.F
720	  q := (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ) V).inv ≫
721	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map x.q
722	  epi :=
723	    @CategoryTheory.epi_comp _ _ _ _ _
724	      (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ) V).inv inferInstance
725	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map x.q)
726	      (@CategoryTheory.Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _
727	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left) inferInstance _ _ x.q x.epi)
728	  locFree := Scheme.Modules.pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank ψ.left x.locFree
729	
730	/-- The pullback action respects the equivalence relation. -/
731	lemma pullbackAlong_rel {T T' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
732	    {x y : LocallyFreeQuotient V d T} (h : x.Rel y) :
733	    (pullbackAlong ψ x).Rel (pullbackAlong ψ y) := by
734	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
735	  refine ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).mapIso f, ?_⟩
736	  change ((pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ) V).inv ≫
737	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map x.q) ≫
738	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map f.hom
739	    = (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ) V).inv ≫
740	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map y.q
741	  rw [Category.assoc, ← (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map_comp]
742	  exact congrArg
743	    (fun m => (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ) V).inv ≫
744	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map m) hf
745	
746	end LocallyFreeQuotient
747	
748	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
749	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
750	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (u+1)` of
751	rank-`d` locally free quotients of a module `V` on a noetherian base `S`
752	(`def:grassmannian_scheme`, [Nitsure] §1, Exercise (2)): an `S`-scheme
753	`T → S` is sent to the set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of rank-`d`
754	locally free quotients `q : V_T ↠ F` (`Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient`), and a
755	morphism to the pullback of quotients.  In the locally noetherian regime
756	this agrees with the Quot-functor special case
757	`Grass(V, d) = Quot^{d, O_S}_{V/S/S}` of the blueprint (a flat finitely
758	presented module with constant fibre dimension `d` is locally free of rank
759	`d`); the locally-free encoding is the form valid over an arbitrary base and
760	the one matched by the merged chart construction
761	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`, `GrassmannianQuot.lean`). -/
762	noncomputable def Grassmannian (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) :
763	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (u + 1) where
764	  obj T := Quotient (LocallyFreeQuotient.setoid V d T.unop)
765	  map {T T'} g := TypeCat.ofHom (Quotient.map (LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong g.unop)
766	    (fun _ _ h => LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong_rel g.unop h))
767	  map_id T := by
768	    ext z
769	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
770	    | _ x =>
771	      change Quotient.mk _ (LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong (𝟙 T.unop) x)
772	        = Quotient.mk _ x
773	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Over.id_left T.unop)).app x.F ≪≫
774	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId _).app x.F, ?_⟩
775	      change ((pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w (𝟙 T.unop)) V).inv ≫
776	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Over.Hom.left (𝟙 T.unop))).map x.q) ≫
777	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Over.id_left T.unop)).hom.app x.F ≫
778	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId _).hom.app x.F
779	        = x.q
780	      rw [Category.assoc,
781	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Over.id_left T.unop)).hom.naturality_assoc x.q,
782	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId _).hom.naturality x.q]
783	      have key := Scheme.Modules.pullback_id_app_coherence (Over.id_left T.unop)
784	        (Over.w (𝟙 T.unop)) V
785	      simp only [pullbackTriangleIso, Iso.trans_inv, Iso.app_inv, Category.assoc]
786	      rw [reassoc_of% key]
787	      rfl
788	  map_comp {T T' T''} g h := by
789	    ext z
790	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
791	    | _ x =>
792	      change Quotient.mk _ (LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong (h.unop ≫ g.unop) x)
793	        = Quotient.mk _
794	          (LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong h.unop (LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong g.unop x))
795	      refine Quotient.sound
796	        ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Over.comp_left _ _ _ h.unop g.unop)).app x.F ≪≫
797	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h.unop.left g.unop.left).app x.F).symm, ?_⟩
798	      change ((pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w (h.unop ≫ g.unop)) V).inv ≫
799	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Over.Hom.left (h.unop ≫ g.unop))).map x.q) ≫
800	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Over.comp_left _ _ _ h.unop g.unop)).hom.app x.F ≫
801	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h.unop.left g.unop.left).inv.app x.F
802	        = (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w h.unop) V).inv ≫
803	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback h.unop.left).map
804	            ((pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w g.unop) V).inv ≫
805	              (Scheme.Modules.pullback g.unop.left).map x.q)
806	      rw [Category.assoc,
807	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Over.comp_left _ _ _ h.unop g.unop)).hom.naturality_assoc x.q,
808	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h.unop.left g.unop.left).inv.naturality x.q]
809	      have key := Scheme.Modules.pullback_comp_app_coherence_inv
810	        h.unop.left g.unop.left (Over.comp_left _ _ _ h.unop g.unop) T.unop.hom
811	        (Over.w g.unop) (Over.w (h.unop ≫ g.unop)) (Over.w h.unop) V
812	      simp only [pullbackTriangleIso, Iso.trans_inv, Iso.app_inv, Category.assoc,
813	        Functor.map_comp]
814	      rw [reassoc_of% key]
815	      simp only [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc]
816	      rfl
817	
818	/-- **Representability of the Grassmannian** (`thm:grassmannian_representable`,
819	[Nitsure] §1 "Construction of Grassmannian"): the functor `Grass(V, d)` is
820	representable by an `S`-scheme.  The merged chart construction
821	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scheme` / `.represents`,
822	`GrassmannianQuot.lean`) proves the absolute case over `ℤ` with `V` free;
823	the relative statement glues the absolute one over a trivialising affine
824	cover of `(S, V)` — the remaining representability endgame of `AJC.picrep`. -/
825	theorem Grassmannian.representable (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) :
826	    ∃ (Y : Over S), Nonempty ((Grassmannian V d).RepresentableBy Y) := by
827	  sorry
828	
829	end Grassmannian
830	
831	/-! ## §5. Representability of the Quot scheme -/
832	
833	/-- **Representability of the Quot scheme** (Grothendieck, Altman–Kleiman;
834	`thm:quot_representable`, [Nitsure] §5): for a noetherian `S`, a projective
835	`π` (encoded: proper and locally of finite type), a line bundle `L`, a
836	coherent `E` and `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`, the Quot functor is representable by an
837	`S`-scheme.  Proof route (Nitsure §5): boundedness by Castelnuovo–Mumford
838	regularity, embedding into a Grassmannian by pushing forward twists,
839	flattening stratification (`AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification`),
840	and the valuative criterion for the closed embedding. -/
841	theorem QuotScheme {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
842	    (π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType π] [IsProper π]
843	    (L E : X.Modules) [L.IsQuasicoherent] (Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
844	    ∃ (Q : Over S), Nonempty ((QuotFunctor π L E Φ).RepresentableBy Q) := by
845	  sorry
846	
847	end Scheme
848	
849	end AlgebraicGeometry
